solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int maxScoreSightseeingPair(int[] values) {
        int maxScore = 0;
        int prev = values[0] + 0;

        for (int j = 1; j < values.length; j++) {
            int currentScore = prev + values[j] - j;
            maxScore = Math.max(maxScore, currentScore);
            prev = values[j] + j;
        }

        return maxScore;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through the array once, maintaining the maximum score of a previous sightseeing spot plus its index. For each new spot, it calculates the score using the previous maximum and updates the maximum score accordingly. This approach ensures linear time complexity and constant space usage.
